task_completion.description: Create the specific scenario directory for Bridge Command
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command (e.g., mkdir) or file manager showing the creation or existence of the directory '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/z) TSS Rogue Intercept/'.
Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the scenario configuration file being edited. It must include settings for the environment 'English Channel East' and visibility greater than 10nm.

task_completion.description: Configure the ownship 'CG Patrol 42' with correct parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the scenario configuration file containing settings for the ownship named 'CG Patrol 42', with Latitude approximately 51.02, Longitude approximately 1.40, heading 225 degrees, and speed 20 knots.

task_completion.description: Configure the rogue cargo ship and the compliant vessel
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the scenario configuration file containing at least two other vessels: a large cargo ship positioned ahead of the ownship heading 045 degrees (acceptable range 20-70), and a compliant vessel heading 225 degrees (acceptable range 200-250).

task_completion.description: Create the intercept briefing document citing COLREGS Rule 10
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/intercept_briefing.txt'. The text must explicitly cite 'Rule 10' and include a description of the target (rogue) vessel.

integrity.description: Verify files are created in the exact requested directories
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal paths or editor window titles to ensure the scenario is saved in '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/z) TSS Rogue Intercept/' and the briefing in '/home/ga/Documents/', not in a temporary or incorrect directory.

integrity.description: Verify the scenario file uses structured configuration syntax
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent is writing actual configuration syntax (like INI format or similar structured text appropriate for a simulator) for the scenario, rather than just writing a plain English summary of the task.

integrity.description: Verify the briefing document is originally written
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the briefing document is written as a realistic maritime briefing and not just a direct copy-paste of the task description prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: COLREGS Rule 10 governs Traffic Separation Schemes (TSS). The Dover Strait TSS Southwest lane heading is approximately 225 degrees, and the Northeast lane heading is approximately 45 degrees. The rogue vessel should have a heading around 45 degrees (acceptable range 20-70), and the compliant vessel should have a heading around 225 degrees (acceptable range 200-250). The scenario files must be located in '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/z) TSS Rogue Intercept/' and the briefing file at '/home/ga/Documents/intercept_briefing.txt'.
